$meta.description: Canonical machine-readable enum of all psychological signal type string keys used in NIKKO. Every array element in a SPEC-100 signal object MUST resolve to a key listed in this file (REQ-100-093). New signal keys require a SPEC-100 revision — they MUST NOT be introduced unilaterally by implementation code.

distress_levels.description: Valid values for the distress_level field in the SPEC-100 signal schema (REQ-100-092). Crosswalk to escalation levels: low=L0, moderate=L1, high=L2, crisis=L3 (GLOSSARY, ratified 2026-05-09).

confidence_bands.description: Numeric confidence band definitions. Apply uniformly across all agents that emit a confidence score (REQ-100-CB1). When confidence falls in the 'low' band, confidence-dependent routing MUST be suspended (REQ-100-CB2).

low.note: Triggers fallback; routing decisions requiring confidence are suspended.

moderate.note: Standard processing; downstream agents adopt softer assumptions.

emotional_states.description: Valid values for the emotional_states array (SPEC-100 §4, REQ-100-030). Represents expressed affect detected in user language — not diagnosed conditions.

cognitive_patterns.description: Valid values for the cognitive_patterns array (SPEC-100 §5, REQ-100-040). Represents thinking styles present in language. MUST inform support strategy only — MUST NOT be interpreted as evidence of illness.

behavioral_indicators.description: Valid values for the behavioral_indicators array (SPEC-100 §6, REQ-100-050). Represents described actions or tendencies. Used to determine readiness for guidance.

risk_indicators.description: Valid values for the risk_indicators array (SPEC-100 §7, REQ-100-060). Highest-priority detection layer. The risk tier is encoded in the key prefix (risk.passive / risk.active / risk.acute) — routing logic MUST parse the prefix to determine escalation severity. Any active or acute key MUST trigger SPEC-300 crisis flow.

passive.description: Indirect risk expression. Escalation rule: passive indicator + distress_level >= high + repetition across >= 2 turns = escalate to L2. A single isolated passive indicator at lower distress does NOT trigger escalation (REQ-100-PR1).

active.description: Direct suicidal or self-harm ideation. Presence of any active indicator MUST trigger SPEC-300 crisis flow immediately (REQ-100-060).

acute.description: Immediate, high-confidence crisis indicators. Highest severity tier. Presence of any acute indicator MUST trigger SPEC-300 crisis flow immediately (REQ-100-060).

support_needs.description: Valid values for the support_needs array (SPEC-100 §8, REQ-100-080). Multiple support needs MAY coexist for a single user turn (REQ-100-080). MUST NOT be treated as diagnoses (REQ-100-070).
